channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Top Hamas military leader killed in Israeli air strikes, official confirms
item.description: Izz al-Din al-Haddad is the most senior Hamas official to be killed by Israel since a US-backed ceasefire agreement meant to halt fighting was implemented in October.

media:group.media:description: A vehicle targeted in one of two Israeli air strikes on Friday intended to kill Hamas's military commander.

item.title: Melbourne City claims ALW championship as Matildas stars shine against Phoenix
item.description: Sensational strikes from young stars Holly McNamara and Leticia McKenna have helped Melbourne City to a record-equalling fifth A-League Women championship.

media:group.media:description: City claimed a fifth championship on Saturday.

item.title: Kohli to play 2027 World Cup only if he can add value
item.description: Virat Kohli says if he plays in the 2027 ICC World Cup he will compete for "every ball like it's the last ball" - but vows if he can't add value to India's team he will not be there.

media:group.media:description: Virat Kohli is only playing in one-day cricket these days, and he says he will only play in next year's World Cup if he is convinced he can add value to India.

item.title: Taylor vows to alter Sex Discrimination Act following 'Tickle v Giggle' ruling
item.description: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor has vowed to amend the Sex Discrimination Act after the Federal Court upheld a landmark ruling that the exclusion of a transgender woman from a female-only app was discriminatory.
item.dc:creator: Lexie Jeuniewic

media:group.media:description: Angus Taylor has vowed to amend Australia’s Sex Discrimination Act to include a definition of biological sex.

item.title: 'Destroys trust in the system': Rally held for Isla Bell
item.description: A rally has been held for Isla Bell in Melbourne after friends and family were left devastated when murder and manslaughter charges were dropped against the man accused of killing the 19-year-old.

media:group.media:description: A vigil was held for 19-year-old Isla Bell, who was found dead in November last year.

item.title: Tasmania to close legal loophole to help child sex abuse survivors
item.description: Tasmania will introduce laws to close a loophole which allows some organisations to avoid being liable for abuse perpetrated by individuals who aren't formal employees.
item.dc:creator: Meg Whitfield

media:group.media:description: Advocate and victim survivor Steve Fisher, from Beyond Abuse, has welcomed Tasmania's newly proposed vicarious liability laws.

item.title: Boy dead, two injured in car crash in north-west Victoria
item.description: Police say a car stolen from Mildura last night was travelling along the Calder Highway when it veered off the road and struck a tree.

media:group.media:description: The crash occurred on the Calder Highway north-west of Bendigo around 10am.

item.title: 'Don't be a d**k, Dezi!': New claims about Freeman police shooting
item.description: The owner of the Porepunkah property where Dezi Freeman shot two police officers says he rang and spoke to Freeman soon after the shooting.
item.dc:creator: Charmaine Manuel

media:group.media:description: Andrew Swift has made new claims in a video recollecting the day police entered his Porepunkah property to arrest Dezi Freeman.

item.title: Origin hopefuls stand up as Cowboys coach hails 'gritty' win
item.description: Todd Payten said his side's victory over the Roosters was "as good a win as I've had" with the club as Melbourne notched back-to-back wins after a messy 34-8 victory over Parramatta and the Sea Eagles thrash Wests Tigers in Magic Round at Lang Park.
item.dc:creator: Simon Smale

media:group.media:description: North Queensland fought back from an early sin binning to stun the Roosters before half time.

item.title: Who was still watching Harvey Weinstein almost a decade on from #MeToo?
item.description: Almost a decade after the #MeToo movement began, disgraced producer Harvey Weinstein's latest trial took place in a new climate. But supporters says its significance remains.
item.dc:creator: Tessa Flemming

media:group.media:description: This April, Harvey Weinstein entered a courtroom again, facing retrial for a 2013 rape charge.

item.title: Stolen car involved in alleged shooting spotted at SA prison staff car park days earlier
item.description: The Public Service Association is calling for urgent and immediate security upgrades after a stolen car allegedly involved in a shooting was seen at the Yatala Labour Prison in the days before the incident.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Mason

media:group.media:description: Police are asking anyone who knows the driver of the vehicle, saw the car, or has dashcam or CCTV footage from the area around the time of the shooting and fire to come forward.

item.title: Trump says the US and Nigeria have killed second-in-command ISIS leader
item.description: US President Donald Trump says American and Nigerian forces have killed the second-in-command leader of ISIS.

media:group.media:description: President Donald Trump says a major ISIS leader has been killed.

item.title: Modi, BJP influence grow in leaps and bounds across India
item.description: How Prime Minister Narendra Modi is turning India into a one-party nation.
item.dc:creator: Tavleen Singh and Bhat Burhan

media:group.media:description: Narendra Modi says the latest election shows India's democracy is vibrant.

item.title: Police make arrest over alleged stabbing of man sleeping at home in NT town
item.description: A 23-year-old man has been arrested over the alleged stabbing of another man who was sleeping in a home in remote Tennant Creek about 990 kilometres south-east of Darwin.

media:group.media:description: NT police have arrested a 23-year-old man in Tennant Creek over the alleged stabbing of another man in town.

item.title: Scottish ref under police protection as Celtic penalty sparks threats
item.description: Scottish FA condemns "vigilantism" after a referee's personal details are leaked online following a highly controversial penalty in the Scottish Premier League.

media:group.media:description: Referee John Beaton caused a storm of controversy by awarding a penalty in added time between Celtic and Motherwell in the Scottish Premier League.

item.title: Kalgoorlie's 'shovel ready' water project misses out in federal budget
item.description: A project designed to drought-proof Australia's biggest outback city has missed out on funding in this week's federal budget, with the City of Kalgoorlie-Boulder set to review its ambitious plans for what has been dubbed Water Bank.
item.dc:creator: Jarrod Lucas and Ivo da Silva

media:group.media:description: Kalgoorlie-Boulder is Australia's biggest outback city with a population of about 30,000 people.

item.title: Live: Blues claim spirited victory over Dogs to begin post-Voss life in style
item.description: Carlton beats the Western Bulldogs by 12 points in its first game since Michael Voss left the club, while Melbourne upsets Hawthorn in style. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Dean Bilton and Luke Pentony

media:group.media:description: Debutant Jack Ison was a bright spark for Carlton, and kicked the game-sealing goal.

item.title: Friends witness 'horrific' shark attack that claimed life of spearfishing mate
item.description: A 38-year-old man had been spearfishing with friends when he was killed in a shark attack off Rottnest Island on Saturday morning.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Williams and Lauren Smith

media:group.media:description: The man was attacked at Geordie Bay on the north of the island.

item.title: Where to watch Delta Goodrem's Eurovision final performance
item.description: The Eurovision Song Contest is on this Sunday, which will mean an early start for superfans of the pop culture phenomenon.
item.dc:creator: Dannielle Maguire

media:group.media:description: Delta Goodrem will be flying the flag for Australia this weekend.

item.title: 'The sword is still hanging': Farmers in limbo as Inland Rail land sits idle
item.description: For more than a decade, Australians in the path of the proposed Inland Rail corridor have wondered if the nation-building project will force them from their homes. Although the northern section has been scrapped, the legacy of its blueprints is still fuelling anxiety.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Long

media:group.media:description: Tim Durre grows crops for hay at his farm in Charlton, Queensland.

item.title: Fury at bill increasing environmental water flows over private property
item.description: Politicians and landholders vow to fight a controversial new New South Wales law that will allow the inundation of private property from environmental water flows under the Murray-Darling Basin Plan.
item.dc:creator: Melinda Hayter, Cara Jeffery, and Nicola Ceccato

media:group.media:description: Deb Blackwell is a grazier at Mundarlo, on the Murrumbidgee River upstream of Wagga Wagga.

item.title: Amid grief, the NT's CLP rushes drastic child protection changes
item.description: Stakeholders have just one week to respond to sweeping changes to NT child protection laws that Aboriginal groups say could do "generations" of harm.
item.dc:creator: Jacqueline Breen

media:group.media:description: Vigils held for Kumanjayi Little Baby have been followed by fierce debate over child protection laws.

item.title: PM says fuel supply now above pre-war levels, no word on extending excise cut
item.description: Australia's fuel supply is now greater than it was before the Iran war broke out in late February, the prime minister has said, while refusing to indicate whether the fuel excise cut will be extended.
item.dc:creator: Annabel Bowles

media:group.media:description: Anthony Albanese says the country's fuel supply is now greater than it was before the Iran war.

item.title: Frustrated Scheffler slams 'absurd' pin positions after disappointing round
item.description: Scottie Scheffler takes issue with the pin positions at the PGA Championship after being upstaged by an American duo.

media:group.media:description: Scottie Scheffler cut a frustrated figure after finishing the second round of the PGA Championshuip tied for ninth.

item.title: The southern Sydney bay residents say is becoming a mudflat
item.description: A bin that has been sticking out of the mud for 12 years has become a symbol for an ongoing issue of sediment build-up in Oyster Bay in Sydney's Sutherland Shire.
item.dc:creator: Declan Bowring

media:group.media:description: Travis Read says the bay has about 15 years before it becomes a mudflat.
